brands.description: Rao's frozen pizzas are an extension of the brand's commitment to authentic Italian flavors, commonly found in grocery stores and specialty food retailers. Known for their thin, crispy crusts, Rao's pizzas are packaged in eco-friendly boxes. They fit into the premium segment of the frozen pizza market, offering a taste of traditional Italian cuisine at home. Distribution channels include major grocery chains and online platforms, making them accessible to a wide audience.
brands.unique_selling_points: Authentic Italian flavors, thin crispy crust, eco-friendly packaging, premium market segment, family-run business
brands.brand_story: Rao's began its journey in 1896 when Charles Rao, an Italian immigrant, purchased a small tavern in New York City. Over the years, the Rao family transformed this establishment into a renowned Italian restaurant, famous for its authentic southern Italian cuisine. By 1992, Frank Pellegrino Sr., a key figure in the family business, decided to share Rao's beloved flavors with a broader audience by launching Rao's Homemade Pasta Sauce. This venture was driven by the desire to offer high-quality, authentic Italian sauces without compromising on ingredients. The brand quickly gained popularity for its commitment to purity, refusing to use sugar, tomato paste, or fillers. As the brand expanded, it introduced a variety of sauces and eventually ventured into other products like soups and dry pasta. In recent years, Rao's has entered the frozen pizza market, bringing its dedication to authentic Italian flavors to a new category. The pizzas are crafted with the same attention to detail and quality that has defined Rao's for over a century. Today, Rao's continues to be a family-run business, maintaining its roots in New York City while expanding its reach across the United States and beyond. The brand's evolution from a small tavern to a household name is a testament to its unwavering commitment to quality and authenticity.

brands.description: Palermo's is a renowned frozen pizza brand known for its authentic Italian-style pizzas. Commonly sold in grocery stores and warehouse clubs, Palermo's offers a variety of crust styles, including their signature thin crust. Their pizzas are packaged in vibrant, eye-catching boxes that highlight the brand's Italian heritage. As a staple in the frozen pizza market, Palermo's is distributed widely across the United States, making it a convenient choice for pizza lovers seeking quality and taste.
brands.unique_selling_points: Authentic Italian-style pizzas, family-owned since 1964, diverse crust styles including thin crust, vibrant packaging, wide distribution across the U.S.
brands.brand_story: Palermo's Pizza was founded in 1964 in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, by the Fallucca family. The brand's origins trace back to a small Italian bakery and pizzeria, where the family shared their passion for authentic Italian cuisine with the local community. The original pizzas were handcrafted, using traditional recipes passed down through generations. As the demand for their delicious pizzas grew, the Fallucca family decided to expand their operations, transitioning from a local pizzeria to a frozen pizza manufacturer. Over the years, Palermo's has evolved significantly, embracing modern manufacturing techniques while staying true to their Italian roots. The brand has expanded its distribution channels, making its products available in grocery stores, warehouse clubs, and foodservice outlets nationwide. Despite its growth, Palermo's remains a family-owned business, committed to delivering high-quality, flavorful pizzas that capture the essence of Italy. The brand's dedication to innovation is evident in its diverse product offerings, including gluten-free and organic options, catering to a wide range of dietary preferences. Palermo's continues to be a beloved choice for pizza enthusiasts, known for its rich history, authentic taste, and commitment to quality.

brands.description: Newman's Own frozen pizzas are a staple in the frozen food aisle, known for their thin and crispy crusts and high-quality ingredients. Commonly found in grocery stores and warehouse clubs across the United States, these pizzas are packaged in recyclable boxes, reflecting the brand's commitment to sustainability. Newman's Own offers a variety of flavors, catering to diverse taste preferences, and stands out in the market for its unique business model of donating 100% of profits to charitable causes.
brands.unique_selling_points: Thin and crispy crust, 100% profits to charity, premium ingredients, recyclable packaging, founded by Paul Newman
brands.brand_story: Newman's Own was founded in 1982 by actor Paul Newman and author A.E. Hotchner in Westport, Connecticut. The brand began with a homemade salad dressing that Newman gifted to friends, which quickly gained popularity. Encouraged by its success, Newman and Hotchner decided to commercialize the product, with the unique twist of donating all profits to charity. This philanthropic model became the cornerstone of Newman's Own. Over time, the brand expanded its product line to include a variety of food items, including frozen pizzas. The pizzas were introduced as part of the brand's commitment to offering high-quality, convenient meal options. Newman's Own pizzas are produced with a focus on using premium ingredients, aligning with the brand's overall ethos of quality and social responsibility. The brand has maintained its independence, resisting acquisition by larger food conglomerates, and continues to operate with the same charitable mission. Today, Newman's Own is a well-respected name in the food industry, known not only for its delicious products but also for its unwavering dedication to philanthropy.

With a focus on quality and flavor, Screamin' Sicilian set out to offer pizzas that were not only delicious but also visually striking. Initially, the pizzas were produced in small batches, using high-quality ingredients sourced from trusted suppliers. Over time, Screamin' Sicilian expanded its product line to include a variety of unique and adventurous flavors, each crafted to deliver a memorable eating experience. As the brand grew in popularity, it expanded its distribution network to include major grocery chains and warehouse clubs across the United States. Despite its growth, Screamin' Sicilian has remained true to its roots, continuing to innovate and delight pizza lovers with its bold flavors and distinctive style.

brands.description: Amy's frozen pizzas are a staple in the organic and vegetarian frozen food market. Known for their commitment to quality ingredients, Amy's offers a variety of pizzas with different crust styles, including gluten-free options. These pizzas are commonly found in grocery stores and health food retailers across the United States. Packaged in eco-friendly boxes, Amy's pizzas cater to health-conscious consumers looking for convenient meal options without compromising on taste or ethics.
brands.unique_selling_points: organic ingredients, vegetarian options, gluten-free crusts, eco-friendly packaging, family-owned since 1987
brands.brand_story: Amy's Kitchen was founded in 1987 in Petaluma, California, by Andy and Rachel Berliner. The brand was born out of a desire to provide organic and vegetarian meal options at a time when such choices were limited. The Berliners named the company after their daughter, Amy, and started with a single product: a vegetable pot pie. Over the years, Amy's expanded its product line to include a wide range of frozen meals, soups, and pizzas, all made with organic ingredients. The brand's pizzas quickly gained popularity for their quality and taste, leading to a significant expansion in production capabilities. Amy's has remained family-owned and continues to focus on sustainability, using solar power in their facilities and supporting organic farming practices. The brand has also expanded its distribution, making its products available in major grocery chains and specialty health food stores across the United States and internationally. Despite its growth, Amy's maintains its commitment to producing wholesome, organic meals that cater to a variety of dietary needs, including gluten-free and vegan options.

brands.description: Home Run Inn is a renowned frozen pizza brand known for its authentic Chicago tavern-style pizza. With a crispy thin crust and all-natural ingredients, it offers a taste of Chicago right at home. Commonly sold in grocery stores and warehouse clubs, Home Run Inn pizzas are available nationwide. The pizzas are packaged in a variety of classic flavors such as cheese, pepperoni, and sausage, catering to diverse palates. As a family-owned business, Home Run Inn maintains its commitment to quality and tradition, making it a staple in the frozen pizza market.
brands.unique_selling_points: Chicago tavern-style thin crust, all-natural ingredients, family-owned since 1947, nationwide distribution, authentic Chicago taste
brands.brand_story: Home Run Inn's story begins in 1923 when Mary and Vincent Grittani opened a cozy tavern on the south side of Chicago. The tavern's name, Home Run Inn, was inspired by a baseball that crashed through one of its windows. In 1947, the tavern began serving its now-famous pizza, crafted with a unique thin crust and all-natural ingredients. The recipe quickly gained popularity, leading to the establishment of Home Run Inn as a beloved pizza brand. Over the years, the company expanded its operations, transitioning from a local tavern to a nationwide frozen pizza brand. Despite its growth, Home Run Inn has remained a family-owned business, preserving its original recipe and commitment to quality. The brand's pizzas are now available in grocery stores and warehouse clubs across the United States, allowing customers to enjoy a slice of Chicago history in their own homes. Home Run Inn's dedication to authentic ingredients and traditional methods has solidified its reputation as a leader in the frozen pizza market.

brands.description: California Pizza Kitchen frozen pizzas bring the innovative flavors of their renowned restaurant to your home. Known for their unique toppings and thin, crispy crust, these pizzas are available in grocery stores and warehouse clubs across the U.S. Packaged for convenience, they offer a gourmet experience in the frozen aisle, setting themselves apart with creative combinations like BBQ Chicken and Thai Chicken.
brands.unique_selling_points: California-inspired flavors, thin crispy crust, innovative toppings, restaurant-quality at home, widely available in grocery stores
brands.brand_story: California Pizza Kitchen (CPK) was founded in 1985 by attorneys Rick Rosenfield and Larry Flax in Beverly Hills, California. The duo sought to bring the casual, innovative dining experience of California-style pizza to the masses, focusing on unique toppings and fresh ingredients. Their first restaurant quickly became a hit, known for its signature BBQ Chicken Pizza. As CPK's popularity soared, the brand expanded rapidly, opening locations across the United States and internationally. In the 1990s, CPK entered the frozen pizza market, allowing fans to enjoy their favorite pizzas at home. This move was facilitated by a partnership with Kraft Foods, which helped distribute the pizzas widely in grocery stores. Over the years, CPK has maintained its commitment to quality and innovation, continually introducing new flavors and improving its frozen pizza offerings. Today, under the ownership of Nestlé, CPK frozen pizzas are a staple in the frozen food aisle, known for their distinctive California-inspired flavors and thin, crispy crusts.

brands.description: Tombstone Pizza is a popular frozen pizza brand known for its bold flavors and classic crust style. Commonly found in grocery stores and warehouse clubs across the United States, Tombstone offers a variety of toppings and sizes. The pizzas are packaged in distinctive, colorful boxes that make them easy to spot in the frozen aisle. As a staple in the frozen pizza market, Tombstone is often chosen for its reliable taste and convenience, making it a go-to option for quick meals and gatherings.
brands.unique_selling_points: Bold flavors, classic crust style, founded in a tavern, colorful packaging, nationwide distribution
brands.brand_story:
Tombstone Pizza was founded in 1962 in Medford, Wisconsin, by brothers Ron and Pep Simek. The brand's origin is as unique as its name, which was inspired by the Tombstone Tap, a tavern the Simek brothers owned that was located across from a cemetery. Initially, the pizzas were crafted in the back of the tavern and sold to local patrons who enjoyed the hearty, flavorful pies. As word spread about the delicious pizzas, demand grew, prompting the Simek brothers to expand their production capabilities.

In the early years, Tombstone pizzas were made by hand, with a focus on quality ingredients and a signature crust that set them apart from other offerings. The brand quickly gained a loyal following, and by the 1970s, Tombstone had expanded its distribution beyond Wisconsin, reaching grocery stores throughout the Midwest.

The brand's growth caught the attention of larger food companies, and in 1986, Tombstone was acquired by Kraft Foods. This acquisition allowed for further expansion and modernization of production facilities, enabling Tombstone to reach a national audience. Eventually, the brand became part of Nestlé's portfolio in 2010, further solidifying its place in the frozen pizza market.

Despite changes in ownership, Tombstone has remained true to its roots, continuing to offer bold flavors and a satisfying crust. The brand's commitment to quality and consistency has ensured its enduring popularity, making it a staple in American households.

brands.description: Totino's is a popular frozen pizza brand known for its iconic Pizza Rolls and Party Pizza. Commonly found in grocery stores and warehouse clubs across the U.S., Totino's offers a variety of crust styles, including its signature crispy crust. The brand is recognized for its convenient packaging, making it a staple for quick meals and snacks. Totino's fits into the frozen pizza market as a go-to option for affordable, easy-to-prepare pizza snacks.
brands.unique_selling_points: Iconic Pizza Rolls, Signature crispy crust, Founded by Rose and Jim Totino, Early entrant in frozen pizza market, Affordable and convenient
brands.brand_story: Totino's was founded in 1951 by Rose and Jim Totino in Minneapolis, Minnesota. Originally starting as a small family-run pizzeria, the Totinos quickly gained popularity for their delicious pizzas. In 1962, they expanded into the frozen pizza market, becoming one of the first to offer frozen pizzas to consumers. Their innovative approach to pizza production and distribution allowed them to grow rapidly. By the 1970s, Totino's had become a household name, known for its unique Pizza Rolls, which were introduced as a convenient snack option. Over the years, the brand has evolved, expanding its product line to include Party Pizza and other snack varieties. In 1975, Totino's was acquired by Pillsbury, which later became part of General Mills. This acquisition allowed Totino's to expand its manufacturing capabilities and distribution network, making it a staple in grocery stores and warehouse clubs nationwide. Despite its growth, Totino's has maintained its commitment to providing affordable and tasty pizza options, making it a beloved brand for generations.

brands.description: Freshétta is a popular frozen pizza brand known for its commitment to quality and freshness. Freshétta pizzas are commonly sold in grocery stores and warehouse clubs across the United States, making them easily accessible to consumers. brands.unique_selling_points: Naturally Rising crust, fresh dough, premium ingredients, diverse crust styles, gluten-free options
brands.brand_story: Freshétta was founded in 1996 with a mission to revolutionize the frozen pizza industry by focusing on freshness and quality. The brand originated in the United States, where it quickly gained popularity for its unique approach to pizza making. Unlike many frozen pizza brands at the time, Freshétta emphasized the use of fresh dough and high-quality ingredients, which resonated with consumers seeking a more authentic pizza experience at home. Over the years, Freshétta expanded its product line to include various crust styles such as Naturally Rising, Brick Oven, and Gluten Free, catering to a wide range of dietary preferences and taste profiles. The brand's commitment to innovation and quality led to its acquisition by Schwan's Sales Co., Inc., a company known for its expertise in frozen foods. This acquisition allowed Freshétta to expand its distribution network, making its products available in grocery stores and warehouse clubs nationwide. Despite its growth, Freshétta has remained true to its original mission of delivering fresh, high-quality pizzas to consumers, maintaining its reputation as a leader in the frozen pizza market.

brands.description: Caulipower is a unique frozen pizza brand that revolutionized the market by introducing cauliflower-based crusts. Commonly found in grocery stores and health food retailers, Caulipower offers a healthier alternative to traditional pizzas. The pizzas are packaged in eco-friendly boxes and cater to health-conscious consumers seeking gluten-free and lower-calorie options. With a focus on quality ingredients and innovative recipes, Caulipower has carved a niche in the competitive frozen pizza market, appealing to those looking for nutritious and delicious meals.
brands.unique_selling_points: cauliflower-based crust, gluten-free, founded by a mother inspired by her sons, eco-friendly packaging, health-focused
brands.brand_story: Caulipower was founded in 2017 by Gail Becker in Los Angeles, California. Inspired by her two sons who have celiac disease, Gail sought to create a gluten-free pizza option that didn't compromise on taste or nutrition. Frustrated by the lack of healthy alternatives in the market, she decided to take matters into her own hands. The initial production was a labor of love, with Gail experimenting in her kitchen to perfect the cauliflower crust recipe. Her dedication paid off, and Caulipower quickly gained popularity for its innovative approach to pizza. As demand grew, the brand expanded its manufacturing capabilities and distribution network, making its products available in major grocery chains and health food stores across the United States. Caulipower's success is a testament to its commitment to providing healthier food options without sacrificing flavor. The brand continues to innovate, introducing new flavors and products to meet the evolving needs of its customers. Despite its rapid growth, Caulipower remains true to its roots, focusing on quality ingredients and a passion for making healthy eating accessible to all.

brands.brand_story: Bernatello's Foods was founded in 1982 in the heart of the Midwest, with a mission to bring high-quality frozen pizzas to families across America. brands.description: Brew Pub Pizza is a bold and hearty frozen pizza brand known for its generous toppings and high-quality ingredients. Featuring award-winning Wisconsin mozzarella cheese, Brew Pub pizzas are available in various styles, including stuffed crust and deep dish. Commonly found in grocery stores and warehouse clubs, these pizzas cater to those with a big appetite and a love for robust flavors. brands.brand_story: Brew Pub Pizza was founded in 2011 in the heart of the Midwest, a region known for its love of hearty, flavorful foods. The brand was born out of a passion for quality and a desire to create a pizza that could stand up to the bold flavors of craft beer, a staple in the local culture. The founders, inspired by the vibrant brewing scene, sought to craft a pizza that was not only delicious but also substantial enough to satisfy the hungriest of appetites. Initially, Brew Pub Pizza was produced in small batches, focusing on using only the finest ingredients, including award-winning Wisconsin mozzarella cheese and high-quality meats and vegetables. As the brand gained popularity, it expanded its production capabilities and distribution network, making its way into grocery stores and warehouse clubs across the country. Over the years, Brew Pub Pizza has remained committed to its roots, supporting local communities and causes. Notably, the brand's Patriot Pizza supports the Tunnel to Towers Foundation, helping provide mortgage-free homes for Gold Star families, first responders, and catastrophically injured veterans. Today, Brew Pub Pizza continues to be a favorite among pizza lovers, known for its generous toppings, unique flavor combinations, and dedication to quality.

brands.description: Orv's is a beloved frozen pizza brand known for its delicious thin crust pizzas. Commonly found in grocery stores across the Midwest, Orv's offers a variety of flavors that cater to diverse taste preferences. Packaged in vibrant, easy-to-store boxes, Orv's pizzas are a convenient choice for families and individuals alike. As part of Bernatello's Foods, Orv's fits seamlessly into the frozen pizza market by providing quality and taste at an affordable price point.
brands.unique_selling_points: Thin crust, Midwest origin, vibrant packaging, diverse flavors, affordable price
brands.brand_story: Orv's Pizza was founded in 1980 in the heart of the Midwest, a region known for its love of hearty, flavorful foods. The brand was the brainchild of Orville Thompson, a passionate food enthusiast who wanted to bring the joy of homemade pizza to the frozen food aisle. Starting with a small production facility, Orv's quickly gained popularity for its unique thin crust and generous toppings, which stood out in a market dominated by thicker crust options. Over the years, Orv's expanded its operations, moving from local grocery stores to larger regional chains, and eventually becoming a staple in households across the Midwest. The brand's commitment to quality and taste led to its acquisition by Bernatello's Foods, a company that shared Orv's dedication to crafting delicious pizzas. Under Bernatello's umbrella, Orv's continued to innovate, introducing new flavors and improving its production methods to ensure consistency and freshness. Despite its growth, Orv's has remained true to its roots, maintaining the same passion for pizza that Orville Thompson instilled in the brand from the very beginning.

brands.description: Roma frozen pizzas are a staple in the frozen food aisles of grocery stores across the United States. Known for their classic thin crust style, Roma pizzas are packaged in vibrant, easy-to-spot boxes that highlight their Italian-inspired flavors. These pizzas are typically sold in grocery stores and warehouse clubs, making them accessible to a wide range of consumers. Roma fits into the frozen pizza market as a reliable, budget-friendly option that doesn't compromise on taste, offering a convenient meal solution for busy families and individuals.
brands.unique_selling_points: Classic thin crust, Italian-inspired flavors, budget-friendly, widely available in grocery stores and warehouse clubs, authentic family recipe
brands.brand_story:
Roma Pizza was founded in 1955 in the heart of the Midwest, a region known for its love of hearty, comforting foods. The brand was the brainchild of an Italian-American family who wanted to bring the authentic taste of Italy to American households. Initially, Roma pizzas were handcrafted in a small family-owned kitchen, using traditional recipes passed down through generations. The founders focused on creating a pizza that was not only delicious but also affordable, ensuring that everyone could enjoy a slice of Italy at home.

As the popularity of Roma pizzas grew, the brand expanded its operations, moving from a small kitchen to a larger manufacturing facility. This expansion allowed Roma to increase its production capacity and distribute its pizzas to a wider audience. Over the years, Roma has maintained its commitment to quality and authenticity, using only the finest ingredients to create its signature thin crust pizzas.

In the 1980s, Roma was acquired by a larger food conglomerate, which helped the brand further expand its reach. With increased resources, Roma was able to enhance its distribution channels, making its pizzas available in grocery stores and warehouse clubs nationwide. Despite these changes, Roma has stayed true to its roots, continuing to offer the same great taste that made it a household name.

Today, Roma is a beloved brand in the frozen pizza market, known for its classic flavors and reliable quality. Its story is one of tradition, family, and a passion for bringing people together over a delicious meal.

brands.description: Giordano's Deep Dish Pizza brings the authentic taste of Chicago's iconic deep dish pizza to your home. Known for its stuffed crust, each pizza is handcrafted in Chicago and flash-frozen to preserve its rich flavors. Available in various pack sizes, Giordano's pizzas are sold online and shipped nationwide, ensuring that pizza lovers across the U.S. can enjoy this culinary delight. The pizzas are packaged in insulated coolers with dry ice, maintaining their freshness during transit. With a focus on quality ingredients, including nearly a pound of mozzarella cheese per pie, Giordano's stands out in the frozen pizza market.
brands.unique_selling_points: Authentic Chicago deep dish, handmade in Chicago, flash-frozen for freshness, nearly a pound of mozzarella per pie, nationwide shipping
brands.brand_story: Giordano's Deep Dish Pizza was founded in 1974 in Chicago, Illinois, by Italian immigrant brothers Efren and Joseph Boglio. Inspired by their mother's recipe for 'Italian Easter Pie,' the brothers sought to bring a taste of their homeland to the bustling streets of Chicago. The original Giordano's restaurant quickly became a local favorite, known for its unique stuffed pizza, which featured a double crust filled with layers of cheese and toppings. Over the years, Giordano's expanded its reach, opening multiple locations across the city and eventually offering nationwide shipping. The brand's commitment to quality and authenticity has remained steadfast, with each pizza still handmade in Chicago by skilled pizzamakers. Giordano's has embraced modern distribution methods, allowing customers to order online and receive their pizzas via UPS and FedEx. Despite its growth, the brand has stayed true to its roots, using top-shelf ingredients and proprietary recipes to deliver the same delicious experience that first captivated Chicagoans over four decades ago.

Originally a small grocery store, the company has grown into a beloved regional supermarket chain known for its commitment to quality and customer service. The Wegmans brand of frozen pizzas was introduced as part of their private label offerings, aiming to provide customers with high-quality, convenient meal options. brands.description: Healthy Choice frozen pizzas are a convenient option for those seeking a healthier alternative in the frozen pizza market. brands.unique_selling_points: Thin crispy crust, eco-friendly packaging, steam-cooked ingredients, health-focused brand, widely available in grocery stores
brands.brand_story:
Healthy Choice was founded in 1989 as a response to the growing demand for healthier food options. The brand originated in the United States, with the aim of providing consumers with nutritious meals that didn't compromise on taste. Initially, Healthy Choice focused on creating frozen meals that were low in fat and calories, using high-quality ingredients to ensure flavor and satisfaction. Over the years, the brand expanded its product line to include a variety of frozen pizzas, each crafted with a focus on health and wellness. 

The brand's commitment to health-conscious eating led to innovations in production methods, such as using steam cooking to preserve the freshness and nutritional value of ingredients. This approach set Healthy Choice apart from traditional frozen meal brands, allowing it to carve out a niche in the competitive frozen food market. 

As consumer preferences evolved, Healthy Choice adapted by expanding its distribution channels, making its products available in major grocery chains and warehouse clubs nationwide. The brand's dedication to providing healthier meal options has remained steadfast, even as it has grown and diversified its offerings. Today, Healthy Choice continues to be a leader in the frozen food industry, known for its commitment to quality, health, and taste.

brands.description: Bellisio Foods is a prominent player in the frozen pizza market, known for its diverse range of high-quality, convenient frozen meals. Their pizzas are commonly sold in grocery stores and warehouse clubs across the United States. Bellisio Foods offers a variety of crust styles and packaging formats, catering to different consumer preferences. The brand is recognized for its commitment to quality and innovation, ensuring that each pizza delivers a satisfying and flavorful experience. brands.unique_selling_points: Diverse range of high-quality frozen meals, strong distribution network, commitment to quality and innovation, founded by industry visionary Jeno Paulucci, established in 1990
brands.brand_story:
Bellisio Foods was founded in 1990 by Jeno Paulucci in Duluth, Minnesota. Originally known for its Italian frozen foods, the company quickly gained a reputation for quality and innovation. Jeno Paulucci, a visionary in the food industry, had a passion for creating delicious and convenient meals that could be enjoyed by families across America. Under Kraft's ownership, Jack's continued to innovate, introducing new flavors and improving its production processes to maintain the high quality that had made it a household name.

In 2010, Nestlé acquired Jack's Pizza as part of its purchase of Kraft's frozen pizza business. Today, Jack's Pizza remains a staple in the frozen pizza aisle, known for its thin crust, affordability, and wide range of flavors. Despite its growth and changes in ownership, Jack's has stayed true to its roots, continuing to deliver the same great taste that Jack Elrick first introduced over 60 years ago.

brands.brand_story:
Udi's began its journey in 1994 in Denver, Colorado, founded by Udi Baron. Initially, Udi's was a small bakery focused on providing delicious, gluten-free baked goods to a niche market. The brand quickly gained a loyal following due to its commitment to quality and taste, which were often lacking in gluten-free products at the time. Udi's original offerings included breads and pastries, which were handcrafted in small batches to ensure the highest quality.

As the demand for gluten-free products grew, Udi's expanded its product line to include frozen pizzas, recognizing the need for convenient meal options for those with gluten sensitivities. The brand's pizzas were initially produced in a small facility in Denver, where Udi and his team experimented with various gluten-free flours and ingredients to perfect their signature thin crust.

Over the years, Udi's continued to innovate and expand its reach. In 2012, the brand was acquired by Boulder Brands, which helped to increase its distribution and visibility across the United States. This acquisition allowed Udi's to scale up its production capabilities and enter new markets, including major grocery chains and health food stores.

In 2016, Conagra Brands acquired Boulder Brands, bringing Udi's under its umbrella. This acquisition further solidified Udi's position as a leader in the gluten-free market, allowing for continued innovation and expansion. Today, Udi's remains committed to its original mission of providing delicious, gluten-free options for consumers, with its frozen pizzas being a staple in many households.

brands.brand_story: Del Popolo was founded in 2012 in San Francisco, California, by Jon Darsky, a former chef with a passion for authentic Neapolitan pizza. The brand began with a mobile pizzeria housed in a repurposed shipping container, bringing artisanal pizza to the streets of San Francisco. This innovative approach quickly gained popularity, leading to the establishment of a brick-and-mortar restaurant. As demand grew, Del Popolo expanded into the frozen pizza market, allowing customers to enjoy their signature pizzas at home. The brand prides itself on using high-quality ingredients and traditional methods to create pizzas that capture the essence of Neapolitan cuisine. Over the years, Del Popolo has maintained its commitment to quality while expanding its distribution to grocery stores and specialty food retailers across the country. Despite its growth, the brand remains rooted in its original mission to deliver exceptional pizza experiences.

brands.headquarter_address: 2768 Rupert Street, Vancouver, BC, Canada
brands.description: Daiya frozen pizzas are a plant-based delight, offering a crisp, thin crust topped with their signature dairy-free cheese and a balanced array of toppings. Commonly found in grocery stores and health food retailers, Daiya pizzas cater to those seeking delicious, allergen-friendly options. Packaged in recyclable boxes, they stand out in the frozen pizza market for their commitment to quality and sustainability. Available across North America, Daiya's pizzas are a staple for vegan and lactose-intolerant consumers.
brands.unique_selling_points: Plant-based, dairy-free cheese, thin crust, recyclable packaging, non-GMO, allergen-friendly
brands.brand_story:
Daiya was founded in 2008 in Vancouver, British Columbia, by Andre Kroecher and Greg Blake. The duo embarked on a mission to create a plant-based cheese that would melt and taste like traditional dairy cheese, addressing the needs of those with dietary restrictions and preferences. Initially, Daiya's products were sold in local health food stores, quickly gaining popularity for their innovative approach to dairy-free cheese.

As demand grew, Daiya expanded its product line to include frozen pizzas, leveraging their expertise in plant-based cheese to create a pizza that was not only vegan but also delicious. The pizzas were initially produced in small batches, but as the brand's reputation spread, production scaled up to meet the increasing demand.

Over the years, Daiya has expanded its distribution network, making its products available in major grocery chains and health food stores across North America. The brand has remained committed to sustainability, using recyclable packaging and ensuring their products are non-GMO.

Daiya's journey from a small startup to a leading name in the plant-based food industry is marked by its dedication to quality and innovation. The brand continues to evolve, introducing new products and flavors while staying true to its roots of providing delicious, allergen-friendly foods that everyone can enjoy.

brands.description: Jakeeno's Frozen Pub Pizzas bring the authentic taste of South Minneapolis to your home. Known for their hearty toppings and flavorful crusts, these pizzas are available in a variety of classic and unique flavors. Sold primarily in local retail locations across Minneapolis and St. Paul, Jakeeno's offers a convenient way to enjoy pub-style pizza without leaving your house. The pizzas come in easy-to-store packaging, making them a perfect choice for quick meals or gatherings.
brands.unique_selling_points: Family-owned since 1975, authentic pub-style pizzas, diverse flavor options, local retail availability, community-focused brand
brands.brand_story: Jakeeno's Pizza & Pasta was founded in 1975 in South Minneapolis by the Keegan family. Originally a small family-run restaurant, Jakeeno's quickly became a local favorite for its authentic Italian cuisine and welcoming atmosphere. Over the years, the restaurant expanded its menu to include a variety of pizzas, pastas, and sauces, all made with fresh, high-quality ingredients. As the demand for their delicious pizzas grew, Jakeeno's decided to venture into the frozen pizza market, allowing customers to enjoy their beloved pub pizzas at home. The brand has remained a family-owned business, now run by the second generation, Patty and Amy Keegan. They have continued to uphold the family tradition of quality and community involvement, partnering with local schools and organizations for fundraising efforts. Jakeeno's has also embraced diversity, proudly supporting the LGBTQ+ community. Today, Jakeeno's Frozen Pub Pizzas are available at select retail locations, bringing the taste of South Minneapolis to homes across the region.

brands.description: Field Day offers a range of frozen pizzas that are known for their commitment to organic and non-GMO ingredients. Commonly found in health-focused grocery stores and natural food markets, Field Day pizzas feature a classic thin crust style. The pizzas are packaged in eco-friendly materials, aligning with the brand's sustainable ethos. Field Day fits into the frozen pizza market as a go-to option for consumers seeking healthier, environmentally conscious alternatives without sacrificing taste.
brands.unique_selling_points: Organic and non-GMO ingredients, eco-friendly packaging, thin crust style, health-focused distribution, commitment to sustainability
brands.brand_story: Field Day was founded in 2005 with a mission to provide high-quality, organic food products at an affordable price. The brand was established in the United States, though the exact city and state of its founding remain less documented. The founders, driven by a passion for clean eating and sustainability, sought to create a line of products that would make organic foods accessible to a broader audience. Initially, Field Day focused on pantry staples and gradually expanded its offerings to include frozen pizzas. The pizzas were originally produced in small batches, emphasizing the use of organic and non-GMO ingredients. Over time, Field Day has grown its distribution network, making its products available in a variety of health-focused grocery stores and natural food markets across the country. The brand has maintained its commitment to sustainability, using eco-friendly packaging and sourcing ingredients that meet stringent organic standards. Field Day's evolution reflects a growing consumer demand for transparency and quality in food production, positioning it as a leader in the organic frozen pizza segment.

brands.description: Against the Grain Gourmet offers a unique take on frozen pizza with its gluten and grain-free options. Known for its small-batch production in Brattleboro, Vermont, the brand uses simple, farm-sourced ingredients to create pizzas that are both delicious and accessible to those with dietary restrictions. Their pizzas are commonly sold in grocery stores and specialty food retailers, packaged in eco-friendly materials that reflect their commitment to quality and sustainability. Against the Grain fits into the frozen pizza market as a premium option for health-conscious consumers seeking alternatives to traditional wheat-based products.
brands.unique_selling_points: Gluten and grain-free, small-batch production, Vermont-based, simple farm-sourced ingredients, eco-friendly packaging
brands.brand_story: Against the Grain Gourmet was founded in 2005 in Brattleboro, Vermont, by Nancy and Tom Cain. The couple, driven by a desire to create delicious gluten-free products, started their journey in a small bakery, focusing on quality and taste. Their mission was to provide gluten and grain-free options without compromising on flavor, a goal that resonated with many consumers. Initially, the pizzas were produced in small batches, using simple, farm-sourced ingredients that everyone could pronounce. As demand grew, the brand expanded its manufacturing capabilities while maintaining its commitment to quality. Over the years, Against the Grain has become a beloved name in the gluten-free community, known for its innovative approach and dedication to customer satisfaction. The brand's evolution included expanding distribution to grocery stores and specialty food retailers across the United States, making their products more accessible to a wider audience. Despite their growth, Against the Grain remains true to its roots, continuing to operate from its Vermont headquarters and emphasizing the importance of community and sustainability in its business practices.

brands.description: Nob Hill Trading Co. offers a range of frozen pizzas that are known for their artisanal crust and gourmet toppings. Commonly found in Raley's and Nob Hill Foods stores, these pizzas are packaged in eco-friendly boxes and cater to those seeking quality and convenience. With a focus on premium ingredients, Nob Hill Trading Co. fits into the niche market of gourmet frozen pizzas, appealing to discerning customers who value taste and sustainability.
brands.unique_selling_points: artisanal crust, gourmet toppings, eco-friendly packaging, premium ingredients, available at Raley's and Nob Hill Foods
brands.brand_story: Nob Hill Trading Co. was founded in 1935 in Sacramento, California, as part of the Raley's family of brands. The brand was born out of a desire to provide high-quality, gourmet food products to the local community. Initially, the brand focused on sourcing and selling premium ingredients, which naturally extended to the creation of their frozen pizza line. The founders, Thomas P. Raley and his family, were committed to maintaining a standard of excellence that would set their products apart in the grocery market. Over the years, Nob Hill Trading Co. expanded its offerings and distribution, becoming a staple in Raley's and Nob Hill Foods stores across California and Nevada. The brand's evolution included a focus on sustainable practices, such as eco-friendly packaging and sourcing ingredients from responsible suppliers. Today, Nob Hill Trading Co. continues to innovate within the frozen pizza market, maintaining its reputation for quality and taste while adapting to modern consumer preferences.

brands.description: Roberta's frozen pizzas bring the authentic taste of Brooklyn's renowned pizzeria to your home. Known for their wood-fired, sourdough crust, these pizzas are crafted by hand and feature housemade dough and mozzarella. Available nationwide through Goldbelly, Roberta's pizzas are a premium choice in the frozen pizza market, offering a gourmet experience that stands out in grocery stores and online platforms.
brands.unique_selling_points: Wood-fired sourdough crust, handcrafted by chefs, nationwide distribution via Goldbelly, Brooklyn origin, housemade dough and mozzarella
brands.brand_story: Roberta's was founded in 2008 in the heart of Brooklyn, New York, by Brandon Hoy, Carlo Mirarchi, and Chris Parachini. What began as a small pizzeria quickly gained a cult following for its innovative approach to traditional pizza-making, featuring a wood-fired oven and a focus on high-quality, locally-sourced ingredients. The founders' commitment to culinary excellence and community engagement helped Roberta's become a beloved institution in New York City. Over time, the brand expanded its reach beyond the confines of its original location, venturing into the frozen pizza market to share its unique flavors with a broader audience. By partnering with Goldbelly, Roberta's has been able to distribute its artisanal pizzas nationwide, allowing fans across the country to enjoy the same quality and taste that made the Brooklyn pizzeria famous. Despite its growth, Roberta's remains dedicated to its roots, maintaining the same handcrafted approach and commitment to quality that has defined the brand since its inception.
